golden_categories.name: Basic URL Validation
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating standard URLs with or without protocols, including basic syntax and structure.

golden_categories.name: Custom Protocols and Schemes
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating URLs with custom or non-standard protocols and schemes.

golden_categories.name: Underscores and Trailing Dots
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating URLs with underscores or trailing dots in the domain.

golden_categories.name: Port Validation
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating URLs with specific port requirements or formats.

golden_categories.name: Protocol and Fragment Options
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating URLs with specific protocol, fragment, or query component requirements.

golden_categories.name: Authentication in URLs
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating URLs with or without authentication information.

golden_categories.name: Host and Domain Validation
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating URLs with specific host and domain requirements, including whitelists and blacklists.

golden_categories.name: Security and Vulnerability Checks
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating URLs against potential security vulnerabilities or malicious patterns.

golden_categories.name: Length Validation
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating URLs based on their length, including maximum length configurations.

generated_categories.name: Default and Basic URL Validation
generated_categories.description: Covers the default behavior and basic validation options for URLs, including standard protocols, host requirements, and optional fragments or query components.

generated_categories.name: Protocol and Port Requirements
generated_categories.description: Validates URLs based on protocol and port requirements, ensuring the presence of these components when specified.

generated_categories.name: Host Validation Options
generated_categories.description: Focuses on host-related validation, including allowing underscores, trailing dots, and whitelist/blacklist configurations.

generated_categories.name: Authentication and Protocol-Relative URLs
generated_categories.description: Validates URLs for authentication components and protocol-relative URLs based on specific options.

generated_categories.name: Length Validation
generated_categories.description: Handles scenarios where length validation is disabled, allowing shorter or empty URLs.

golden_to_generated.reason: Both categories focus on basic URL validation, including standard protocols, host requirements, and optional fragments or query components.
Both categories emphasize validation of URLs with specific protocol and port requirements.
GENC-3 includes host-related validation options like underscores and trailing dots, which partially overlap with GC-3's focus on underscores and trailing dots.
GENC-2 covers port requirements, which partially align with GC-4's focus on port validation.
GENC-1 includes optional fragments and query components, which partially overlap with GC-5's focus on protocol, fragment, and query options.
Both categories focus on authentication components in URLs.
GENC-3 includes host-related validation options like whitelist/blacklist configurations, which partially overlap with GC-7's focus on host and domain validation.
No generated category explicitly focuses on security and vulnerability checks.
Both categories focus on length validation for URLs.

generated_to_golden.reason: GENC-1 overlaps with GC-1 on basic URL validation and partially with GC-5 on optional fragments and query components.
GENC-2 strongly matches GC-2 on protocol requirements and partially matches GC-4 on port validation.
GENC-3 partially overlaps with GC-3 on underscores and trailing dots and with GC-7 on host validation options.
GENC-4 strongly matches GC-6 on authentication components in URLs.
GENC-5 strongly matches GC-9 on length validation for URLs.

missing_golden_categories.reason: No generated categories achieved strong or partial semantic overlap with this fixed golden category.
